emails.subject: Re: Local Production for June
emails.body: Re: Local Production for June If the deal is confirmable as a deal that came across on CES's books [reported as an active deal by CES to ENA], then the deal is a CES deal (CES Statoil). BUT, if this is a newly discovered deal - - we will need to confirm with Risk (Mark Breese) to ensure proper booking that the deal has been quantified; OR if it is a newly negotiated deal or a deal finalized after 1/1/00, then it should be entered without the CES prefix (statoil). Based on the production dates of deal# 280185 and #226563, as combine with the fact and that I can not find anything in the CES deal listing (CES 'books') specifying a 333/day deal with Statoil I am again hesitant to use the CES prefix (nor can I confirm that deal #226563 should have been entered with the CES prefix. John, can you fill in the missing details? Cyndie Chris Germany@ECT 05/25/2000 04:19 PM

emails.subject: Re: Local Production for June
emails.body: It is definitely a CES deal that will be over in Sep or Oct of this year. Cyndie Balfour-Flanagan@ENRON 05/25/2000 04:52 PM
